content_markdown:
Two identical sources dipping in phase into water in a ripple tank generate the wave pattern shown below.

Along which of the labelled lines is the difference in path length from the two sources equal to **two** wavelengths?

diagram_metadata.description: A ripple tank interference pattern showing two point sources and several radial lines labelled A, B, C, and D. The central dashed line represents the zero-order maximum. Line A is the first nodal line, line B is the first-order maximum, line C is the second nodal line, and line D is the second-order maximum.

vibe_explanation: In an interference pattern from two in-phase sources, constructive interference (maxima) occurs when the path difference is an integer multiple of the wavelength ($n\lambda$). The central line is $n=0$. The first line of constructive interference (maxima) to the side is $n=1$ (line B), and the second line of constructive interference is $n=2$ (line D). Lines A and C represent destructive interference (nodal lines) where the path difference is $(n + 1/2)\lambda$.
